mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 3.63% represents an elevated level in a tightening cycle. Sustained FEDFUNDS above 3.5% signals continued monetary restriction, which increases real discount rates and compresses long-duration asset valuations. This upward pressure on yields persists over the next 20 trading days as market participants price in the restrictive stance.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.3% is near cyclical lows, indicating tight labor market conditions. Continued low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) signals persistent wage pressure and inflation risk, forcing the Fed to maintain or raise rates, which drives 10-year Treasury yields upward as inflation expectations embed into long-term rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.0%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines despite UNRATE < 4.5% and inflation above 2.0%, or the joint signal fails to predict yield direction in > 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1465K units and retail sales at $757B indicate robust demand conditions. Strong demand proxies signal economic resilience and inflation persistence, prompting expectations of higher-for-longer rates. This sustained demand backdrop pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher as growth expectations remain elevated.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points when both HOUST > 1400K and RSAFS > 750B, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 102.65 (2017=100) shows supply-side strength. A sustained increase in INDPRO signals productive capacity expansion and potential disinflationary pressure. However, if INDPRO growth slows below 0.5% month-over-month while demand remains strong, supply constraints emerge, pushing yields higher as inflation expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below 0.3% while RSAFS remains above 750B
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines when INDPRO growth is below 0.3%, or the signal fails to predict yield direction in > 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 159,001K persons shows strong labor market momentum. A month-over-month payroll gain exceeding 150K signals continued economic expansion and wage pressure. This persistent employment strength sustains inflation expectations, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 12 basis points despite PAYEMS growth remaining positive, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at $24,152.656B (Q2 2017) represents solid growth. A quarterly GDP growth rate above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that justifies higher long-term rates. This growth backdrop supports the Fed's tightening bias and pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward as investors demand higher compensation for duration risk.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days despite GDP growth above 2.5%, or the signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 333.979 (1982-1984=100) reflects cumulative inflation. If month-over-month CPI growth accelerates above 0.3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%, a policy lag emerges. Market participants anticipate aggressive Fed tightening to close this gap, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as forward rate expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls when CPI growth > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS < 4.0%, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production and payroll employment jointly signal supply-side tightness. When INDPRO growth slows to below 0.2% month-over-month while PAYEMS continues to grow above 100K monthly, a supply-demand imbalance emerges. This mismatch raises inflation expectations and forces higher long-term rates as the Fed must tighten further to restore equilibrium.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.2% and PAYEMS monthly gain > 100K
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 15 basis points when INDPRO growth < 0.2% and PAYEMS > 100K, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
